The myocardial lineage formed a distinct cluster of cells represented by the cluster "Myocardium" which could be distinguished by the expression of myl7, cmlc1, myh6, myh7, tnnt2 (Fig.2A, Supplementary Table 3) [60,61], as well as the RFP transgenic marker (Supplementary Fig. 1C).

The "AV cushion" differs from the "AV endocardium" cluster by the presence of myocardial-associated genes (myl7, ttn.2, and a number of mitochondrial genes) and a higher expression of cell adhesion and extracellular matrix proteins (postna, col1a1a/b/2) (Supplementary Table 3).

The "AV cushion" differs from the "AV endocardium" cluster by the presence of myocardial-associated genes (myl7, ttn.2, and a number of mitochondrial genes) and a higher expression of cell adhesion and extracellular matrix proteins (postna, col1a1a/b/2) (Supplementary Table 3).
